description: Depending on a coarse umbrella target couples unrelated code so any small change forces a large, slow rebuild.
body: Failure mode: a target lists a broad dependency to avoid thinking about exact needs, so editing one file invalidates many downstream actions. Detection signals: a tiny edit rebuilds far more than the touched code; bazel query shows the target reaching unrelated libraries; build times stay high despite caching. Do not depend on umbrella targets for convenience. Safer fallback: split coarse targets along real dependency lines, depend only on what is used, and verify with bazel query that the rebuild scope shrank.
